event.summary: Claimed one ready iteration unit without expanding repository or path scope.

acceptance.proof: A reviewed owner descriptor and resolved lock open only the bounded UDP discovery effect; absent or mismatched activation remains inert and rejected.

One caller-supplied loopback bind/destination exchanges an accepted query and admits bounded response envelopes under explicit nonzero datagram, response-count, receive-slice, and total-deadline limits.

Pre-send cancellation, bounded receive cancellation/timeout, malformed or oversized datagrams, response-count exhaustion, and socket release have deterministic behavioral tests and stable typed outcomes.
The runtime selects no interface or endpoint, claims no official interoperability/currentness, and grants no Manifold authority.

document.commit_policy: Commit the closed activation artifacts, bounded runtime implementation, and behavioral evidence coherently before validation; keep acceptance and push preparation separate.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the first explicit bounded network activation and retain endpoint and Manifold exclusions.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the full owner, runtime-loopback, public-boundary, feature-lock, and workflow gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Describe the opt-in bounded discovery runtime without broader interoperability claims.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the full owner and public-boundary gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Document socket ownership, explicit activation, cancellation, cleanup, and authority boundaries.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the full owner and runtime-loopback gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Separate Rust loopback runtime evidence from official-endpoint interoperability evidence.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the public-boundary and runtime-loopback gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route proportional behavioral, timeout, cancellation, cleanup, and negative validation.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the declared Standard validation profiles.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review network/public routing; no shared-skill mutation expected.
Review explicit runtime effect, cleanup, and authority separation; no shared-skill mutation expected.

non_scope: Interface enumeration, ambient endpoint selection, multicast membership joins, broadcast policy, DNS, retries, background threads, asynchronous runtimes, or persistent sockets.

Official-endpoint interoperability, reachability, ecosystem compatibility, query-response correlation/currentness, provider identity, clock synchronization, outlet/inlet handshake, sample transport, buffering, recovery, or runtime defaults.
Dependencies, unsafe, FFI, devices, private artifacts, inbound-sample commands, or Manifold admission, registry, session, stream-state, route, topology, authorization, replay, or audit authority.
document.objective: Open the first explicit pure-Rust LSL runtime effect as a caller-configured, dependency-free bounded UDP discovery exchange: one supplied bind address and destination, one accepted short-info query, finite datagram/count/deadline and cancellation bounds, borrowed parsing where possible, deterministic socket cleanup, and loopback behavioral evidence.
outputs: Closed owner descriptor/project selection and resolved lock for only the bounded UDP discovery effect.
Dependency-free explicit UDP discovery API with finite limits, typed outcomes, cancellation, and cleanup.
Loopback behavioral and damaged-path tests plus synchronized public routing and proportional Standard validation evidence.
